ingredients.note: 1.750 kg for hot preparation; 1.800 kg (approx. 5% more) for cold preparation
1 L for hot preparation; 1 kg for cold preparation

steps_en: Heat the sugar and water mixture to boiling.
Alternatively, prepare cold using approximately 5% more sugar (1.800 kg sugar to 1 kg water), without heating.
notes_en: Hot preparation is preferred over cold. Addition of 100-200 g citric acid per quintal of sugar is recommended to prevent crystallization. This concentrated syrup (57 L water per quintal sugar) weighs 157 kg with sugar content of about 93-94%, and can be stored in barrels then diluted with water as needed. Sugar content limits for liqueur classes: Rosoli/Creams 350-450 g/L, Fine Liqueurs 175-325 g/L, Ordinary Liqueurs 50-115 g/L, Aqua vitae 40-85 g/L.
